text.title: JUnit 5 + Spring Boot Test Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for layered Spring Boot tests with JUnit 5, Mockito, Testcontainers.

rule.statement: Controller tests MUST be annotated `@WebMvcTest(YourController.class)` and inject `MockMvc`. Service collaborators MUST be `@MockBean`. `@SpringBootTest` is forbidden for controller-only tests.
rule.rationale: `@WebMvcTest` boots only the MVC layer тАФ milliseconds vs seconds for `@SpringBootTest`.

rule.statement: Service unit tests MUST use `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` + `@Mock` + `@InjectMocks`. Manual constructor calls (`new OrderService(new MockRepo())`) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: `MockitoExtension` enforces strict stubbing + auto-resets mocks between tests, catching divergence between setup and code.

rule.statement: Repository tests MUST use `@DataJpaTest` to load only the data layer. Custom `@Query` + Specification predicates + lazy-loading paths MUST be tested. Eager-load contracts MUST use `JOIN FETCH` or `@EntityGraph`.
rule.rationale: `@DataJpaTest` spins up only `EntityManager` + repositories тАФ fast, focused, and rolls back per test.

rule.statement: Integration tests MUST use `@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = RANDOM_PORT) + @Testcontainers + @Container` static Postgres (or production DB engine). H2 in-memory MUST NOT be used when the code depends on relational constraints, JSONB, sequences, or vendor-specific SQL.
rule.rationale: H2 silently passes tests that production fails on (different SQL dialect, missing JSONB, no real FK enforcement).

rule.statement: Boundary + validation tests MUST use `@ParameterizedTest` + `@ValueSource` / `@CsvSource` / `@MethodSource`. Duplicated `@Test` methods for parametric inputs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: `@ParameterizedTest` reports each input as a separate test result; duplicated `@Test` hides which case failed.
